fields.notes: Kitchen close. New special: braised short rib.
Closing lead. Holiday party of 30 in private dining.
Kitchen close. Private party plated dinner service.
Post-holiday reset. New Year specials wrap up.
Moved to AM-only per scheduling accommodation request.
Valentine's reservations — fully booked through Saturday.
Kitchen close. Valentine's Eve — special tasting menu prep.
Valentine's Day. All hands on deck.
Closing lead. Valentine's Day — expect highest revenue night.
Lunch prep and dinner line lead.
Confirm large-party reservations for the week.
Closing lead. Fish fry night — expect heavy cash volume.
Kitchen close. Lenten fish fry prep — St. Boniface order.
Closing lead. Alvarez quinceañera prep — private dining room.
Day bar. Maria takes over PM.
Thanksgiving dinner special — prix fixe menu.
Holiday party of 30 in private dining.
Holiday season. Strong bookings all week.
Peak holiday Saturday. Highest revenue night of December.
Christmas Eve early seating. Closed at 9 PM.
New Year's Eve prix fixe dinner. Full house.

Drawer short $180. Carmen said large party paid cash and she may have given incorrect change. Checked receipts — party total was $347, paid with $400, correct change would be $53. Doesn't account for full shortage.

Largest shortage yet. Carmen attributed to a voided transaction on table 12. Checked void log — void was $42 comp for undercooked steak, not $340. No other voids on record for this shift. Sunday night, lower foot traffic than usual. Carmen was covering Daniela's usual Sunday close.

Friday close. Carmen said new server (Sofia) may have miscounted change on a cash table. Spoke with Sofia — she only had 2 cash tables totaling $89, all reconciled on her server report. Shortage unaccounted for.
Carmen off — Daniela covered Friday close.

Saturday night — busiest shift of the week. $420 short. Carmen had no explanation other than 'crazy night.' Reviewed camera footage: camera angle doesn't cover register drawer clearly. Need to request better angle from security vendor.
Valentine's Day. Record revenue night. All reconciled perfectly.

Smaller shortage — Thursday, slower night. Carmen mentioned she had to break a $100 bill for a customer's take-out order. Register tape shows the transaction but drawer count is still off. Pattern is consistent: only short on Carmen's closes.

Saturday again. I counted the drawer myself at 3 PM shift change — was accurate to the penny at that point. Shortage occurred between 3 PM and close. Only Carmen had register access after 9 PM when last server clocked out.
Lenten fish fry — strong cash night. All reconciled.

Running total now $2,000+. Cross-referenced every shift where Tony or Daniela closed instead of Carmen (6 shifts since Jan 1) — all reconciled perfectly. The pattern is undeniable. Need to talk to Sandra before next step.

Largest single shortage. $2,400 total across 8 incidents, all on Carmen's closes. Documentation is thorough. Contacted Sandra — meeting scheduled next week. Cannot act until caregiving replacement for Mom's evenings is identified. This is the constraint.
Walk-in slightly elevated. Heavy delivery day — door opened frequently during Thanksgiving week restock.
Checked PM — walk-in came back down.
Walk-in slightly warm. Holiday prep overloading walk-in — reorganized shelving.
Walk-in slightly warm — Valentine's Day prep filling capacity.
PM check — walk-in stabilized after dinner service prep moved to stations.
Stocked fish fry delivery — walk-in near capacity.
Walk-in door seal looks worn — flagged for Victor.

Walk-in reading 42°F — above 40°F safe limit. Compressor fan cycling intermittently. Notified Victor. Moved perishable proteins to freezer as precaution. Door seal may be compromised from heavy Saturday traffic.
Walk-in came back down overnight after compressor reset. Still above ideal. Victor called refrigeration service — Monday appointment scheduled.
Refrigeration tech came this morning. Replaced compressor relay and tightened door gasket. Holding at 36°F. Invoice: $485.

fields.special_requirements: Private dining room + outdoor terrace. Tres leches cake from external bakery — coordinating delivery. Mariachi band arrives 7 PM, need rear entrance cleared.
fields.notes: AT BELLA NOTTE — full private dining buyout plus terrace overflow. Final headcount confirmed 3/15. Family-style service, traditional Mexican-Italian fusion menu. Florist delivering centerpieces 3 PM day-of.

fields.special_requirements: 150 portions per Friday. Sysco standing order for cod fillets (case of 40 lbs weekly). Parish hall delivery by 4 PM each Friday.
fields.notes: Standard fish fry menu: beer-battered cod, coleslaw, fries, rye bread. Week 5 of 7 complete. Feedback from Father Nowak: congregation loves it. Remaining Fridays: Mar 27 and Apr 3. Deposit covers first 4 weeks; invoicing remaining weeks as delivered.

fields.special_requirements: Intimate dinner, main dining room buyout. Japanese-Italian fusion tasting menu — Tony developing 7-course concept. Wine pairing: Robert at Capital Wine selecting Barolo and sake pairings.
fields.notes: Need final menu approval by 3/28. Dr. Nakamura has 2 guests with severe shellfish allergies — flagged for kitchen. Sommelier-guided pairing for each course. Budget allows premium selections.

fields.special_requirements: Off-site at the museum. Passed hors d'oeuvres + 3 stations (pasta, carving, dessert). Need 8 servers + 2 bartenders. Setup access starts 2 PM day-of.
fields.notes: Linen & Things confirmed 40 round tablecloths. Sysco special order: 30 lbs beef tenderloin, 20 lbs shrimp. Museum contact: Sarah Chen, Events Director. AV handled by museum. Valet parking included in museum package.

fields.special_requirements: Full restaurant buyout, 6:30–10:30 PM. Northern Italian prix fixe — 4 courses plus cocktail hour. AV requirements for CEO speech. Premium wine service.
fields.notes: Contact: Lisa Johnson, VP Operations. Budget allows premium wine service. Deposit covers 50% of estimated $17K total. Need to finalize AV requirements — podium, mic, projector screen. Second payment due April 15.

fields.special_requirements: Cocktail-style reception, heavy appetizers + open bar. Bar needs to stock extra prosecco and local craft beer (Lakefront Brewery).
fields.notes: Menu draft sent to event coordinator Karen Whitfield 3/10 — awaiting feedback. Expecting response by early April. 200 guests may require full restaurant + patio. Need to confirm tent rental if weather uncertain.

fields.special_requirements: Private dining + patio. Senior leadership dinner. Sommelier-led wine education segment with Maria. Dietary restrictions survey sent to 60 attendees — awaiting responses.
fields.notes: Contact: David Bernstein, VP Events. High-end experience expected — this is their top-tier leadership group. Discussing 5-course tasting menu with wine pairings. Budget is generous. Final dietary restriction responses due by May 15.

fields.special_requirements: Full restaurant + patio buyout. Ceremony at Cathedral of St. John — reception starts 5 PM. Bride (Lisa Chen) wants family-style Italian with Sichuan-inspired appetizer course. Florist delivering 3 PM.
fields.notes: Still finalizing cake vs. dessert table. Second deposit installment due May 1. Coordinator: Lisa Chen's mother, Lisa Chen's mother — (414) 555-0841. Need to confirm DJ setup location and dance floor arrangement by April 15.
